slip.anonymizedFrom: Anonymized real-world Singapore broker placement slip (Test D)

_min_score_comment: Conservative floor; raise to 0.92 once extraction is wired in Phase 0 final and we have a real baseline number to anchor on. Wider grandComputedPct (5%) than other fixtures because the GHS/GP/SP bundling makes computed totals inherently fuzzy.

client.address:
Main Location 1: 4 Test Lane, Singapore 100004
Location 2: 5 Test Industrial Park #01-01, Singapore 100005
Location 3: 6 Test Logistics Hub, Singapore 100006

_policyEntities_comment: Slip lists 3 entities under 'Insured': Test D Pte Ltd (SG main, master policy), Test D China Ltd, Test D Americas LLC. The wizard's PolicyEntity model handles this multi-jurisdiction case via isMaster + sibling rows.
_insurers_comment: Three insurers: Tokio Marine Life (7 sheets but 5 products after GHS aggregation), Berkshire (GPA + WICA — slip uses two label variants 'Berkshire' and 'Berkshire Hathaway', extractor should canonicalize to one code), Chubb (GBT). Berkshire is NOT in the current seed catalogue — Phase 1 follow-up.
_products_comment: 8 logical products (after collapsing 3 GHS sheets into 1). The cross-sheet GHS aggregation is the headline test for this fixture. GHS-Locals declares '(Premium includes GP & SP)' — extractor should flag potential bundling overlap with the standalone GP/SP product totals.

products._categories_comment: GTL has 2 categories: Board of Directors (36x salary) and All Others incl. Secondees (24x salary). Both compulsory.

products._aggregation_comment: GHS spans 3 sheets (Locals + Secondees + Dependants). The extractor must aggregate these into ONE Product. Locals premium 182348 'includes GP & SP' per slip note — flag potential double-count with standalone GP/SP entries below.
products._plans_comment: GHS uses 6 cover-tier plans (B3, B2, B1, B, A, A1) with per-employee rates (Phase 1 introduces basis='per_employee').

products._bundling_comment: GP standalone shows 230474. Slip says GHS-Locals premium 'includes GP & SP'. Either GP is bundled (declaredPremium effectively 0 for billing) or both are billed (potential double-count). Extractor should surface this as a warning.

products._bgt_comment: Group Business Travel — annual flat premium per region/plan, not per-employee. Single plan A.

products._wica_comment: 10 employee categories with per-class rates. Berkshire Hathaway label variant should canonicalize to BERKSHIRE.
products._plans_comment: Each category gets its own rate. Test asserts plan count, not individual codes (too verbose to enumerate).

reconciliation._grand_comment: Two interpretations of grand total because of the GHS/GP/SP bundling: (a) all premiums summed naively = 1,015,646 (likely double-counts GP+SP). (b) GP+SP excluded (treated as bundled into GHS-Locals 182348) = 638,130. Allow wide range to accept either interpretation.
